prompt: Design a literary Chinese book cover in a refined minimalist collage style on warm off-white textured paper with subtle grain and faint aging. The composition is tall and elegant, with one dominant central-right vertical Chinese title in large distressed black calligraphy-style characters reading “你当像鸟飞往你的山”, arranged in 2 vertical columns, with the right column reading “你当像鸟” and the left column reading “飞往你的山”. A delicate indigo-blue bird flies near the top center, and a thin winding blue dotted flight path snakes diagonally down across the title and toward the lower right, creating a sense of ascent and freedom. In the lower left, place a large triangular mountain form like a torn paper collage or painted wedge, its peak white and snowy, fading into deep ultramarine and navy textures below. Integrate a faint vertical strip of tiny English book text along the mountain’s right edge, as if the mountain is partially constructed from printed pages. Inside the blue mountain, set the original English title in serif type: “Educated” on one line and “A Memoir” below it, in white. On the upper left, add a short vertical block of small Chinese quote text in black, arranged as 6 lines, with a long dash below, then the author name “Tara Westover” in muted blue serif on 2 lines, followed by another dash. Along the lower right edge, add slim vertical credits in small type: Chinese text for the author followed by the English name “Tara Westover” in blue, then translator and publisher lines beneath in black. Keep the layout spacious with strong negative space, asymmetrical balance, and a poetic contemporary publishing aesthetic. Use only 3 main colors: black, off-white, and indigo blue. The overall feeling should be intellectual, restrained, modern, and award-winning, suitable for a premium edition of Educated by Tara Westover.
